title: Selective reductions hide quiet promotion-race defenses
reference_note: The bad king move lets Black's connected passed pawns take over. Stockfish triage scored the bad move about 416 cp worse than a rook-file defense; the fixed engine no longer repeats f4g4 at the traced depth.

before.baseline: pre-fix baseline commit d611f85, before the passed-pawn rank fix and selective-reduction guard

fix.summary: Passed-pawn blocker detection now uses real board rows, and late move pruning / late move reductions are skipped in sparse promotion-race nodes where quiet blockade moves are tactically decisive.

root_cause.summary: The verifier reproduced the traced depth-8 f4g4 decision five times. The original position isolated late move pruning at depth 8, while the color-swapped variant isolated late move reductions at depths 6 and 8. Both heuristics assumed late quiet moves were disposable in a sparse endgame even though quiet rook and king blockade moves are the critical defense against near-promotion pawns.
